sections.paragraph:
δώρημα, ατος, τό (Aeschyl., Hdt. et al.; IG IV2, 128, 53 [c. 280 b.c.] δ. of Asclepius; Kaibel 1039, 13; Sir 34:18; Ezk. Trag. 35 [in Eus., PE 9, 28] θεοῦ δ.; Philo, Somn. 1, 103 δ. παρὰ θεοῦ) gift, present δ. τέλειον a perfect gift Js 1:17 (Philo, Abr. 54 δ. τέλ.; EpArist 276 θεοῦ δ. καλόν; Jos., Ant. 4, 318 δ. κάλλιστον; PSI 29, 33 τἀγαθὰ δορήματα). οὐχ ὡς διʼ ἑνὸς ἁμαρτήσαντος τὸ δ. the gift (of grace) is not like the effects of one man’s sin Ro 5:16; ἐκ τῶν ἰδίων δ. from the gifts that oneself has received Hm 2:4. δ. τοῦ κυρίου Hs 2:7.—DELG s.v. δίδωμι B. M-M. TW.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

Kaibel Kaibel = Epigrammata Graeca ex Lapidibus Conlecta, ed. GK.—List 3

Ezk. Ezk. Trag. = Ezekiel the Tragedian, II b.c.—List 5

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

Hm Hm = Mandates

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 266
